# Contributing to Qafiyah

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Qafiyah. This document covers local development setup, issue and pull request workflows, and project conventions.

## First Contribution

If this is your first time contributing to open source, the [How to Contribute to Open Source](https://opensource.guide/how-to-contribute/) guide is a useful starting point. Issues labeled `good first issue` or `documentation` are good entry points.

## Prerequisites

- Bun (enforced via the `packageManager` field; npm, yarn, and pnpm are blocked by a preinstall hook)
- Docker (runs the Postgres container on port 5433 plus Elasticsearch; the dev/up scripts manage them)
- Git

## Local Development Setup

1. Fork the repository and clone your fork locally.
2. Install dependencies:
   ```bash
   bun install
   ```
3. Start everything in development mode (Docker must be running):
   ```bash
   bun run dev
   ```
   This boots the seeded Postgres and Elasticsearch containers (Postgres auto-restored from the latest dump on first boot; the worker reindexes Elasticsearch if empty), writes local `.env` files, and starts the web app and API with hot reload.
4. To run only the API:
   ```bash
   bun --filter @qafiyah/api run dev
   ```

The web app is served on port 4321 and the API on port 8787. For port-conflict errors, run `bun run clean` and try again.

## Environment Variables

`bun run dev` writes local environment files automatically (copying `.env.example` files if missing):

- `apps/api/.env`: `DATABASE_URL` pointing to the local Docker Postgres instance
- `apps/web/.env`: `PUBLIC_API_URL` pointing to the local API on `:8787` (so browser islands query the local DB without CORS)

Do not commit these files; they are listed in `.gitignore`.

## How to Contribute

### Issues

Report bugs, propose features, or ask questions via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/alwalxed/qafiyah/issues). Include enough detail to reproduce the problem or evaluate the proposal.

### Pull Requests

1. Fork the repository and clone it locally.
2. Create a branch for your changes (see [Branch Naming](#branch-naming)).
3. Implement your changes following the existing code style and conventions.
4. Ensure all tests pass and add new tests if applicable.
5. Submit a pull request with a clear description of the change and the motivation.

## Branch Naming

Use lowercase kebab-case prefixed by the change type:

| Prefix      | Use case           |
| ----------- | ------------------ |
| `feat/`     | New features       |
| `fix/`      | Bug fixes          |
| `docs/`     | Documentation only |
| `refactor/` | Code refactoring   |
| `chore/`    | Maintenance tasks  |

Example: `feat/search-filters`, `fix/bot-retry-logic`

## Priority Areas for Contribution

Contributions are particularly valuable in the following areas:

- Accessibility improvements
- Security enhancements
- Code quality and refactoring
- Documentation improvements
- UI/UX enhancements
- Bug fixes and issue resolution
- Data quality and accuracy

## Commit Messages

Commit messages are enforced via a commit-msg hook ([Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)). Every commit (except merge commits) must follow this format:

- **Format**: `<type>(<scope>): <subject>`, e.g., `feat(api): add poem search endpoint`
- **Allowed types**: `feat`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `test`, `perf`, `build`, `ci`, `revert`, `wip`, `merge`, `hotfix`, `deps`, `config`, `lint`, `types`
- **Scope** (optional): Add in parentheses after the type, e.g., `fix(bot):`
- **Breaking changes**: Append `!` after the type or scope, e.g., `feat(api)!: rename poem slug field`. Include a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer in the commit body describing what changed and how to migrate.
- **Subject**: Use imperative mood, e.g., "add user profile", not "added" or "adds"
- **Length**: The entire commit message must not exceed 280 characters
- Merge commits generated by Git (e.g., "Merge branch ...") are exempt from these rules

## Running Tests

```bash
bun run test                            # run all workspace tests
bun --filter @qafiyah/api run test      # API tests only
vitest run path/to/file.test.ts         # single file
```

Tests use [Vitest](https://vitest.dev/). The pre-commit hook (`bun run ci`) runs format, lint, type-check, test, knip, and madge before every commit; the suite must pass locally before a commit is accepted.

## Pull Request Checklist

Before submitting a pull request, verify the following (TRUST 5 quality criteria):

- [ ] **Tested**, `bun run test` passes; new logic has test coverage
- [ ] **Readable**, zero lint errors; names are self-explanatory
- [ ] **Unified**, code matches Biome config; commits follow Conventional Commits
- [ ] **Secured**, no secrets committed; inputs are validated at boundaries
- [ ] **Trackable**, the PR description explains _why_ the change was made

## Guidelines

- Follow the project's [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).
- Test your changes thoroughly before submission.
- Update relevant documentation to reflect changes in functionality or usage.
- Keep pull requests focused on a single feature or fix.
- Respond to review feedback in a timely manner.

## Questions and Support

For questions or assistance, open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/alwalxed/qafiyah/issues) or contact **alwalxed@proton.me**.

Thank you for contributing to Qafiyah and helping preserve Arabic literary heritage.
